Ordspillet og den moderne lyrik: Dansk resume English Summary: Traces of Fire. The Pun and Modern Poetry In poetry, puns are much more than just humour and jokes. They are fundamental to the very formation of the

They are fundamental to the very formation of the poetic function of language. This dissertation therefore not only focuses on the more or less entertaining aspects of this element when poetry is read, but also argues that they are elementary to the way in which poetry is created. The aim of this dissertation is primarily to expos

The aim of this dissertation is primarily to expose several ways in which the pun is used in modern poetry and to explain how the different applications of the pun can be placed in a poetological context, where it is connected to other poetic phenomena such as, in particular, the metaphor. Another of its aims is to give Scandinavian reader

Another of its aims is to give Scandinavian readers an account in Scandinavian – for the first time since Albeck's scanty overview from 1940 – of the ways in which puns have been used at various times throughout literary history. In a diachronic perspective, the dissertation distinguishes between the use of puns as a critique and as a sort of magic. I argue that these two uses are caused by the way

I argue that these two uses are caused by the way in which puns enter into language, it being possible to focus on either a semiotic deficit or a semantic surplus. Reversibility makes itself felt with regard to both the use of puns and the distinctive attitude to language underlying the play on words itself. In principle, a scale of usage ranging from a crit

In principle, a scale of usage ranging from a critique of language to magical language can be divided indefinitely, but in this dissertation I have chosen to focus on three relevant modernistic strategies used by three, roughly speaking, contemporary authors who each follow their own strategy or poetology. 1) In the first strategy, language and the world are confused with each other in puns because language is sensed before it is understood. But the wording "confused with" already implies a

But the wording "confused with" already implies a sceptical attitude towards language since it suggests a difference beoe a similarity between world and language. 2) In the second, the formulation is softened by saying that language and the world are exchanged in puns. 3) And in the third, the formulation becomes totally fluid in that the world and language are fused together in puns. These formulations form a register or a scale whic

These formulations form a register or a scale which spans from a critique of language to magical language use: from puns in opposition to metaphor to puns as a kind of metaphor. I shall use  Traces of Fire. The Pun and Modern Poetry: English Summary they are considered part of the 1) differentiation, 2) intensification or 3) extension of language.  poetry uses puns. With this separate section on t

poetry uses puns. With this separate section on three contemporary Danish poets – Per Højholt, Peter Laugesen and Simon Grotrian – my aim is to demonstrate that it is possible to draw parallels to other authors, domestic as well as foreign. I have chosen to devote the majority of space in t

I have chosen to devote the majority of space in this section to the poems of Per Højholt because it is my opinion that his poems and particular oeuvre contain more, and not least more essential, reflections on the subject of puns as well as related figures and genres and what they can do in and for poetry. The dissertation begins by introducing puns in literature and the three authors' particular use of them, and it attempts to show three different ways in which they are used by the three authors. I define puns as a joining of two or more semantic

I define puns as a joining of two or more semantic fields through similarities on the semiotic level, and at the end of the introduction I delimit them in relation to neologisms. In neologisms the play on words has transcended its semiotic limits; the play is hypostasized at the semantic level and is fused with association and other kinds of similarities. As a result, it is necessary to take into account

As a result, it is necessary to take into account at least two perspectives in order to describe puns, because they can be conceived as both semiotic and semantic phenomena. In other words, there is an interaction between th

In other words, there is an interaction between these two understandings of puns, and the use of puns is often dependent on what is emphasized by this interaction or interplay: whether it is the semantic hesitation between various senses or its semiotic insufficiency – the idea that there is only one expression This interaction is the reason for viewing puns according to their function, and this is my main argument in chapter one, the starting point of which is "dead language", especially clichés. From clichés and their relationship to the pun, I

From clichés and their relationship to the pun, I turn to how the shape of words can have an effect on the way language is produced, and thus its sense as well. In other words, I focus on  are joined to show the ways in which puns, through so-called interaction, have a revitalizing effect on language, bringing new life to "dead language". Puns have this effect because they are sensible, while "dead language" remains unsensed language Traces of Fire. The Pun and Modern Poetry: English Summary Chapter

The Pun and Modern Poetry: English Summary Chapters two and three review the emergence of puns and their role in literary history until today. My analysis is based upon articles and books on puns concerning either a specific period or the work of a specific author. History shows us different ways to consider and look at puns and word plays at various times. In some periods puns are very unpopular; in others they are much more appreciated. Regardless of the period, puns are tied up with ot

Regardless of the period, puns are tied up with other phenomena such as sophism in Plato, rhetoric for the Romans, wit in the Age of Enlightenment, irony for the Romantic Movement, and the unconscious from the beginning of the twentieth century. They rarely appear in clean shape, so to speak; they are always involved in a more or less poetological context, either through the history of ideas and/or literary history. Still, two general functions of the pun become app

Still, two general functions of the pun become apparent, and they can be described as either a critique of language or magical language. Magical language is not the same for the Greeks as it is, for instance, for Walter Benjamin or Simon Grotrian, but it is nevertheless a mark of an often unheard-of confidence in language. The same goes for the critique of language, the broad outline of which is as rough as that drawn for magical language. My argument is that puns have a special role in th

My argument is that puns have a special role in the emergence of modernistic literature and modern literary theory, not a leading role, but a function which corresponds very well with several of the tendencies and ideas that can be observed from the beginning of the century: from Freud and Saussure to Russian formalism and Chapter four gives an account of four categories within the secondary literature on puns and wordplays. Whereas chapters two and three read this secondary

Whereas chapters two and three read this secondary literature within a diachronic perspective, chapter four reads it with a view to creating a broad theoretical overview and synthesis of what puns accomplish in literature. Based on this secondary literature I conclude that we do not always grasp the sensation or semiotic deficit of the pun, but that we instead form a kind of semantic surplus through, for instance, metaphors. Metaphors offer a way to comprehend a deficit in l

Metaphors offer a way to comprehend a deficit in language, and the pun is an example of such a deficit, making it explicit. This is one of the reasons why they play such a big part in poetry, but they also have a poetic function in language as such. Puns make us aware of how it feels mentally when we let go of our consciousness (for instance, through various forms of distraction such as a sneeze), but Traces of Fire. The Pun and Modern Poetry: English Summary the men

The Pun and Modern Poetry: English Summary the mental slip is remarkable and brings about, for example, metaphoric language or metaphors, Chapter five consists of two parts, the first of which discusses rhyme as related to puns, and the second compound words as related to puns. The two parts lead to the classification of puns a

The two parts lead to the classification of puns as contributing to differentiating, intensifying or extending language, a classification which structures the order of the three following chapters (six, seven and eight) on the three different authors: Højholt, Laugesen and Grotrian. In chapter five, puns are gradually put in a broader context and in a perspective of interpretation. In this way the readings of the three authors' wor

In this way the readings of the three authors' works are not just readings of the function of puns in the works of each author, but also readings of the particular oeuvre as such. Chapter five furthermore shows how and in which ways neologisms and portmanteau words are different. Portmanteau words are more intimately related to t

Portmanteau words are more intimately related to the narrow definition of puns mentioned above and they are primarily a pre-modern device and invention from the nonsense tradition (Lewis Carroll), which is overtaken by avant- However, puns must continuously be read and interpreted in a broader context, as is demonstrated in the chapters on the individual oeuvres. In Højholt's literature, puns serve a parodic func

In Højholt's literature, puns serve a parodic function as well as to generally differentiate the language, which in my view is characterized by a concept of hesitation throughout his works: in the smallest elements of his poems as well as inscribed autobiographically on the level of his oeuvre. In Laugesen's oeuvre, puns are mainly used in the form of rhyme and with an intensifying result; with puns and rhymes Laugesen tries to intensify the language as much as possible. Puns ignite language in Laugesen's poetry, akin to

Puns ignite language in Laugesen's poetry, akin to stoking the fire in a steam engine. Puns are, for Laugesen, part of a potential reservoir of nonsense which can be used to revitalize language. Grotrian uses these in yet another and more enigmatic way, but behind the riddles and enigmas it is possible to sense a reality that looks different after having read his poems. His poetry makes reality more palpable and more meaningful, although this reality is often merely sensed or assumed. In Grotrian's poetry puns are not a possibility or

In Grotrian's poetry puns are not a possibility or a potential that can be chosen, but a poetological condition. Frequently, the result is that his poems approach or become a kind of song (of thanksgiving Traces of Fire. The Pun and Modern Poetry: English Summary  sentences signify because the meaning is not the purpose, which is beyond meaning; it is what  sentences' way of signifying, is sacrificed for a religious purpose. Chapter nine, the concluding chapter of the disser

Chapter nine, the concluding chapter of the dissertation, offers a summary and a brief discussion of the economy of puns as regards semantics and semiotics. I emphasize again the importance of the above-mentioned interplay of the semiotic deficit and the semantic surplus, or that the critique of language and magical language are each others' dialectical opposites. A critique of language does not exist without magical language and vice versa. This relation is caused by a logical and linguisti

This relation is caused by a logical and linguistic necessity: puns give rise to and are used in a shift of perspective between the semiotic and the semantic. Puns make language speak; they at once appeal to and address us in a special way. Initially, understanding or communication in the play on and with words does not need to be present. But then this is precisely what we laugh about: that our comprehension has nothing to grasp. This could be called a corporal understanding or c

This could be called a corporal understanding or comprehension which has an intellectual sequel or postlude. We sense puns before we fully understand them. This is the reason why the French philosopher Gilles Deleuze's concept of "sens" is fundamental to this dissertation and its premise. The French word "sens" (sense), which in itself is

The French word "sens" (sense), which in itself is a pun on sensation, meaning and direction, captures the interplay between sensation and meaning, and also the point that their relationship is not always clear and certainly not unidirectional. Rather, they are interconnected in a highly complicated way that leads to both epiphanic clear-sightedness and Babylonian confusion. Traces of Fire. The Pun and Modern Poetry: English

headline_text: Shfaqja e Antropologjisë në Shqipëri: Shkrime të zgjedhura në 60-vjetorin e lindjes, përgatitur nga Enika Abazi [The Advent of Anthropology in Albania: Selected essays on the 60th birth anniversary, edited and introduced by Enika Abazi]
text:
Një dorë manushaqe të egra për mendime të thella
në studimet shqiptare. Askush nuk ka shkaktuar më shumë
shkëndija intelektuale për të nxitur me kaq elegancë imagjinatën
e re kërkimore shkencore në Shqipëri. « I dashur zoti Doja, ju keni një njohuri të plotë dhe bëni një prezantim të thelluar... Edhe njëherë
leximi i shkrimit tuaj më lejoi të mat shtrirjen e diturisë dhe rigorozitetin, si dhe rëndësinë e
dialektikës tuaj... Sa i përket Derridas... duke e ditur vendin e jashtëzakonshëm që ai ka fituar në
universitetet amerikane, ju jam mirënjohës për gux

universitetet amerikane, ju jam mirënjohës për guximin dhe për qartësinë tuaj për t'i vënë gjërat
në vendin e duhur.» «Nuk mund veçse të habitesh thellësisht nga shkalla e madhe e vëzhgimeve të gjera të Albert
Dojës... Shpesh ai tregon një ndjeshmëri etnografike thuajse të shndritshme...» «Diskutimi i tij mbi metodën është shumë interesant dhe pasqyron rrjedhën e formimit të tij.
tekstuale dhe hulumtime mbi dokumentet arkivore, si dhe metodën krahasuese... Një pikë e fortë
në hulumtimet e tij tregon rëndësinë e antropologj

në hulumtimet e tij tregon rëndësinë e antropologjisë për të kuptuar konfliktet bashkëkohore në
rajonin e Ballkanit.» Jonathan Friedman, Shkolla e Studimeve të Avancuara në Shkencat Sociale në Paris (EHESS) & Universiteti i Kalifornisë në San Diego
«Antropologjia e Albert Dojës rimerr mësimet e historisë së disiplinës për të kuptuar se si ajo
bëhet një antropologji në njëfarë mënyre sociologjike dhe politike, e cila merr parasysh aspektet Albert Doja
- Anëtar i Akademisë së Shkencave të Shqipërisë

ethical foundation, Aristotle, Nicomachean Ethics, A Study of the Ethical Foundation of Terminal . Care in Health Care Abstract : The 'terminal care' is one of the most important and urgent problems in health care. Human nature is not only a mortal existence. but also a suffering-from-illness existence. Everyone is destined to pass into death through illness. Every human being dies through 'terminal' stage. Therefore we are reqvired what to think of the terrpinal care. The taking care of the terminal patients is really

The taking care of the terminal patients is really a hard job to medical professionals and their family members at the same time. But none of them can evade it, for what the patients are today reflects what we shall be tomorrow. In order to.endure the hard task in terminal care, what spiritual and ethical background can we expect? To our astonishment we can find in Aristotle's IVicomachean Eth ics a really great and attractive Philia theory to support our terminal care. Philia is love which treats even with our friends

Philia is love which treats even with our friends as same as with ourselves. With philia, we could regard our terminal patients as if they were equal to ourselves. Philia might be one of the greatest foundations Key Words:terminal care, health care, patients, ethical foundation, Aristotle, こういつた項目の中で徐々に深刻の度合を増しているのは,ターミナル・ケア(Terminal Care) れた現代医療に関する意識調査において,ターミナル・ケアは他の項目(脳死と植物状態,臓
器移植,体外受精)とくらべて相対的に高い関心の的となっていた。このことからだけでなく, れは恐ろしい

器移植,体外受精)とくらべて相対的に高い関心の的となっていた。このことからだけでなく, れは恐ろしい 何よりも恐ろしいことである。身の毛がよだつほどおそろしい」と感じ,「生命
飢餓状態に陥る」ことが少なくないだろう。 2 ターミナル・ケアの困難さ
 医療の現場からのターミナル・ケアの実践報告が多いことはすでに述べたが,このことはと 年にシシリー・ソンダース(Dame Cicely Saunders)によってロンドン郊外に設立されたセン
ト・クリストファー・ホスピス(St. Christopher's Hospice)は,現代のホスピスの象徴的存 在であるが,わが国でも末期ガン患者の介護の施設として浜松市の聖上三方原病院の病棟の一
部に,聖隷ホスピスが昭和56年に開設された。ホスピスはターミナル・ケア専門の施設として われているにとどまる。わが国におけるホスピス存立の困難さは経済的な事由によるだけにと
どまらないのである。 医師)は「治療」に極めて熱心であり,ついで「延命」に情熱を注ぐ。治療の段階を過ぎた患
者,あるいは必ずしもそれが最善の選択とは言い難い患者に治療を行なおうとしたり,.延命第 がある。もと

者,あるいは必ずしもそれが最善の選択とは言い難い患者に治療を行なおうとしたり,.延命第 がある。もとよりターミナル患者の全てに病状告知が一律に行なわれるべきだ,との杓子定規
は退けられるべきであろう。よりよいケアのためには,原則として医療者と患者とが真実に基 わが国の医学教育と看護教育に死を迎えようとしている患者に対応するための教育が欠けて
いて,早急に是正されるべきだとの指摘は,関係者によってしきりと繰り返されている。末期
